"vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:L/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-400", "name": "Uncontrolled Resource Consumption"}], "description": "Summary
`decimal` doesn't bound the exponent on parsed input, so something like `"1e10000000"` is parsed fine but then explodes the memory to more than 7GB if you run e.g. `Decimal.add(Decimal.parse("1e10000000"), 1)` because for positive `exp`, the function tail-recurses with `coef * 10` and `exp - 1` per iteration, growing the bignum coefficient by one digit each step. In the worst case, one request is enough to OOM the BEAM.

### Details
`Decimal.new/parse/cast` happily store huge exponents. After that, a bunch of core paths allocate proportional to `exp`:
- `add/sub/div` go through `add_align`, which calls `pow10(exp1 - exp2)` and builds a giant bignum (lib/decimal.ex:1734-1738, 1827).
- `to_string/2` with `:normal` (also `:xsd` and the `String.Chars` impl) does `:lists.duplicate(exp, ?0)` (lib/decimal.ex:1506, 1513).
- `to_integer/1` recurses `coef * 10`, `exp - 1` once per unit of `exp` (lib/decimal.ex:1603-1605).
- `round/3` does the same `:lists.duplicate` trick on the exp difference (lib/decimal.ex:1850, 1874).
- `compare/3` with a threshold argument loops back into `add`/`sub`, so it's vulnerable too (lib/decimal.ex:331-332).

### PoC
Any of these will hang or OOM the BEAM:
```elixir
Decimal.add(Decimal.new("1"), Decimal.new("1e1000000000"))
Decimal.to_string(Decimal.new("1e1000000000"), :normal)
Decimal.to_integer(Decimal.new("1e1000000000"))
Decimal.round(Decimal.new("1e1000000000"))
```

### Impact
Unauthenticated remote DoS. Anything that takes a user-supplied decimal (JSON, form field, Ecto `:decimal` field — basically everywhere) and then does arithmetic, rounding, `to_integer`, or `to_string` on it is exposed. One request can kill the node with a Out-of-Memory exception.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00321, "percentile": 0.23647}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-12T15:09:20Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-32686"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-07T15:16:05Z", "published_at": "2026-05-12T15:09:20Z", "references": ["https://github.com/ericmj/decimal/security/advisories/GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-32686", "https://github.com/ericmj/decimal/commit/6a523f3a73b8c9974540e21c7aa88f1258bb35ae", "https://cna.erlef.org/cves/CVE-2026-32686.html", "https://github.com/ericmj/decimal/releases/tag/v3.0.0", "https://osv.dev/vulnerability/EEF-CVE-2026-32686", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/ericmj/decimal/security-advisories/GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/ericmj/decimal", "summary": "Decimal: Unbounded exponent in `Decimal.new` enables unauthenticated DoS", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-12T15:09:21Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-rhv4-8758-jx7v", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.0.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "erlang", "name": "decimal"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.1.0, < 3.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Decimal: Unbounded exponent in `Decimal.new` enables unauthenticated DoS
